pr.title: run permuter on all remaining low-mismatch functions
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Permuter-assisted matching pass across 34 low-mismatch files, spanning camera, fighter, game mode/results/menu, stage/ground, interface, library, map, player, and VI code. The PR body says the author sorted remaining functions by mismatch count, had Codex run a permuter on many of them, and reverted data-layout-related matches such as inline-string swaps until whole translation units are ready to link. The accepted changes are mostly small source-shape adjustments for matching: helper inlines, register-allocation nudges, explicit globals/externs, struct typedefs with size assertions, constant spelling changes, pointer arithmetic rewrites, assertion spelling, include pruning, and a few corrected function/global references.
what_changed: Ran a broad permuter/Codex cleanup over remaining low-mismatch functions, producing 330 added and 286 deleted lines across 34 files.

Introduced small static inline helpers to isolate source regions and improve matching, including ftCo_800AFC40_inline0, ftCo_Bury_GetTranslate, fn_80179350_update, mn_8022BE34_OnEnter, mnName_8023A9B4_GetUserData, mnName_8023A9B4_ResetDisplayOrder, and mnName_8023A9B4_GetGObj.

Added register-allocation and optimizer-control nudges, such as #pragma global_optimizer off around ftCo_LightThrowDash_Phys and lbSnap_8001DF20, plus a deliberate empty cd != NULL block noted as needed for register allocation.
Changed several constant spellings and expression forms for matching, including replacing 1 / 256.0f with 0.003906f in ftaction.c and reordering multiplication in ftCo_LightThrowDash_Phys.

Pruned many unnecessary includes from ftKirby/ftKb_Init.c and changed Kirby special-N callbacks from the generic ftCo_8009D704 call to specific ftCo_8009D074/8009D18C/8009D2A4/8009D3BC/8009D4D4/8009D5EC/8009D920/8009DA38 calls.

Added or reshaped data declarations where useful for matching, such as gm_1832_8047368C_t with STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...) == 0x24), gm_80186F6C_Entry, grMaterial_MObjInfo, initialized soundtest static arrays with { 0 }, and extern sdata2 floats in ifcoget.c/soundtest.c.

Adjusted assertion source forms, including HSD_ASSERT(..., NULL) to HSD_ASSERT(..., 0), HSD_ASSERTREPORT text change in granime.c, and explicit __assert calls in gricemt.c, grmutecity.c, and groldkongo.c.

Made small local-variable and pointer-expression reshapes for matching: selected_slot in camera.c, copied_dst in gm_16AE.c, created_text/color_word names in gm_18A5.c, pointer arithmetic in gmmain_lib.c, cast reuse in lbcollision.c, and for-loop form in mplib.c.
Changed header signature pl_80037C60(Fighter_GObj*, volatile s32 prev2070_int), indicating a volatile parameter was necessary for matching or ABI/source-shape behavior.
smart_moves: The author explicitly used mismatch counts to prioritize work, which is a good triage method for harvesting easy decomp wins.
Permuter output was not accepted blindly: the PR body says data-layout-related wins such as swapping inline strings for explicit variables were reverted when they should wait for whole-TU linkage.
Source-shape fixes were kept small and localized, often by extracting an inline helper instead of rewriting surrounding logic.
Optimizer pragmas were scoped with #pragma push/#pragma pop, limiting global_optimizer off to only the functions that needed it.
The diff preserves readability where possible by replacing obvious permuter slop names with descriptive local names such as created_text, text_color_word, copied_dst, selected_slot, next, and cur.
When modeling data, the PR used explicit typedefs and STATIC_ASSERTs rather than anonymous byte blobs only, improving future reverse-engineering utility.
decomp_lessons: For low-mismatch functions, source shape often matters more than logic: inline extraction, variable lifetime changes, expression ordering, and constant spelling can close gaps.
Do not accept data-layout fixes opportunistically if they depend on broader translation-unit layout; defer risky inline-string/global swaps until the TU can be linked and verified.
Scoped #pragma global_optimizer off can be a valid last-mile tactic for register allocation, but it should be narrowly applied and documented.
Explicit helper inlines are reusable for forcing compiler structure while keeping the top-level function readable.
Use address-commented externs and named structs when a global’s layout is known enough to improve matching, but pair structs with STATIC_ASSERTs when size is important.
Literal spelling can be significant: 0.003906f generated different code from 1 / 256.0f in several ftaction.c call sites.
Assertion wrappers can affect matching; HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTREPORT, NULL versus 0, string literals, and direct __assert calls are all source-shape levers.
Header qualifiers can matter: adding volatile to a parameter prototype in pltrick.h is evidence that signatures may need exact qualifier modeling for matches.
naming_conventions: Inline helper names commonly derive from the parent function plus a descriptive suffix, e.g. ftCo_800AFC40_inline0 and fn_80179350_update.
Getter helpers use Get-prefixed descriptive names, e.g. ftCo_Bury_GetTranslate, mnName_8023A9B4_GetUserData, and mnName_8023A9B4_GetGObj.
One-off entry helpers can use OnEnter suffixes when matching setup paths, e.g. mn_8022BE34_OnEnter.
Recovered struct typedefs used file/function/global-based names such as gm_1832_8047368C_t and gm_80186F6C_Entry.
Address-commented globals remain important, e.g. /* 4DA78C */ extern s32 lbl_804DA78C and /* 4DDC20 */ extern float un_804DDC20.

When the real symbol name is still address-like, a macro alias can preserve a readable name while matching storage, e.g. #define devtext_drawlist un_804D6E18 and #define light_dist_func grMc_804D46CC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a script to sort candidate functions by mismatch count before running a permuter.
Run the permuter on small low-mismatch targets and manually filter the resulting source changes.
Extract exact code blocks into static inline helpers to alter inlining and register allocation without changing behavior.
Apply #pragma global_optimizer off only around functions where optimizer behavior blocks a match.
Insert harmless-looking source constructs when needed for register allocation, as in ftCo_ItemThrow.c's cd != NULL block with an explanatory comment.
Change arithmetic association/order, such as (cd->x404 * friction) * cd->x40C instead of cd->x40C * (...).
Spell constants as compiler-friendly literals, such as 0.003906f instead of 1 / 256.0f.
Replace direct struct field access with accessor calls where matching requires it, e.g. HSD_JObjGetTranslationX(jobj) instead of jobj->translate.x.
Model globals as extern sdata2 values when literals likely reside in small data, as in ifcoget.c and soundtest.c.
Use explicit pointer arithmetic and casts to force address-calculation codegen, as in gmmain_lib.c.
Use volatile in prototypes where needed to preserve codegen, as in pl_80037C60.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
The only rationale text is from the PR body: many data-layout-related matches were reverted because the author felt that work should wait until the whole translation unit is ready to be linked.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue the low-mismatch permuter weekend work mentioned by lukechampine.
Check whether the deferred data-layout-related inline string/global swaps were later restored when full translation units linked.
Search for other uses of #pragma global_optimizer off to identify accepted patterns and limits.
Audit functions touched here for whether helper inlines were later renamed or removed after larger TU matching.
Verify whether ftKb_Init's specific ftCo_8009Dxxx calls reflect known action-specific callbacks and whether related headers were updated elsewhere.
